title: AGENTS.md
text: erplots provides fluent mini-language building exposure-response plots: model curves/ribbons, quantile-binned response-rate/mean summaries, raw-data layer, grouped distribution panels. model-agnostic: erplots never fits model . model implementing er_predict() can visualised; implementing er_simulate() er_summary() additionally enables uncertainty spaghetti plots/VPCs summary annotations (e.g. p-values). See ?er_model_interface. er_plot() takes response_type = c("auto", "binary", "continuous", "count") argument (auto-detected response column given: logical values entirely {0, 1} → "binary", else "continuous"; "auto" never resolves "count" – must declared explicitly), stored object$response$type/ object$response$limits. model curve/ribbon (er_plot_show_model()) group panel (er_plot_show_groups()) layers work response type dispatch needed. quantile summary layer (er_plot_show_quantiles()) er_vpc_plot() now fully generalised across three response types (rate + Clopper-Pearson "binary"; mean + t-interval "continuous"; mean + exact Poisson interval "count"). data layer (er_plot_show_data(), renamed er_plot_show_datastrip()) also works response type, "continuous"/"count" error path left – see “Planned work” . er_plot_show_data() offers two mutually exclusive styles: "overlay" (default) plots raw observations true (exposure, response) coordinates directly model panel – single, response-type-agnostic builder (build_data_overlay()) small vertical jitter applied binary responses, color aesthetic ’s always strata (sharing base plot’s legend) rather response value. "jitter" older panel-based design (build_data_jitter() binary response, build_data_color() continuous/count, object$part$data) – continuous/count response produces one color-coded panel (one per stratum level) base plot instead overlay main panel. two styles live separate object$part slots (overlay vs. data); setting one via er_plot_show_data() clears . companion package erglm (formerly erlr) fits GLM-based exposure-response models implements package’s generics model objects. erplots hard dependency erglm – ’s listed Suggests, examples/tests/vignettes. Since erglm GitHub-(CRAN), DESCRIPTION also declares Remotes: djnavarro/erglm CI can resolve . Key API points remember: example dataset erglm::erglm_data (erlr::lr_data), models fit erglm::erglm_model(formula, data, family = ...) (family explicit). erglm now genuinely supports binomial/poisson/gaussian/Gamma families, matching binary (ae1/ae2), count (ae_count), continuous (biomarker_change, ae_duration) response columns erglm_data – just binomial. Models simulated erglm::erglm_vpc_sim(); er_predict()/er_simulate()/ er_summary() methods erglm registers model objects correct four families, erplots-side changes needed .

title: AGENTS.md
text: Every er_plot_show_*() function (er_plot_show_model(), er_plot_show_quantiles(), er_plot_show_data(), er_plot_show_groups()) takes builder argument (er_plot_show_model() additionally takes summary_builder) lets user supply function matching standard build_*() signature (function(data, config, stratify, exposure, response, strata, style)) place whatever style string otherwise select – e.g. geom_crossbar()-based quantile builder instead built-ins, density/histogram-based data-layer builder instead scatter. first-class, documented part API (see ?er_partial’s “Writing builder” section), internal implementation detail – previously worked undocumented escape hatch (reassigning object$part$<layer>$config$builder directly), since .build_*_geoms() always calls whatever function lives config$builder regardless got . data layer, style still selects structural family ("overlay": single call merged main panel; "jitter": one--panels stacked base plot, per object$response$type) custom builder slotted ; three layers ’s one structural call site, style ignored builder supplied. also resolved old TODO customising model layer’s summary annotation without breaking style arg (now summary_builder). er_plot_show_quantiles()’s style now two built-options: "errorbar" (default, build_quantile_errorbar()) "pointrange" (build_quantile_pointrange(), single geom_pointrange() place separate point + error bar). build_quantile_pointrange() started hypothetical example builder escape hatch promoted built-style option since needed new config fields beyond .part_quantile() already computes build_quantile_errorbar().

title: AGENTS.md
text: R/er-generics.R – model interface: er_predict(), er_simulate(), er_summary() generics default methods. R/er-plot-api.R – public plot-building API: er_plot(), er_plot_show_model(), er_plot_show_quantiles(), er_plot_show_data(), er_plot_show_groups(), er_plot_build(), plus print/plot methods er_plot S3 class. layer function (except er_plot() ) dedicated Rd topic (shared @rdname). R/er-plot-part.R – internal .part_*() functions assemble configuration plot component (er_predict() / er_simulate() / er_summary() get called user-supplied model). R/er-plot-build.R, R/er-plot-compose.R – internal plotting/layout machinery turns parts ggplot2 objects composes patchwork. R/er-plot-partials-*.R – pluggable build_*() partial builders (one file per component: model, summary, quantile, data, group). See ?er_partial interface builders share. R/er-vpc.R – er_vpc_plot(), model-agnostic VPC-style plot operating plain observed/simulated data frames. R/utils-helpers.R, R/utils-global.R – small internal helpers (including binary-response-clopper_pearson(), t_interval(), poisson_interval(), cut_quantile(), cut_exposure_quantile(), response-type detector .detect_response_type()) globalVariables() declarations NSE.

title: erplots development plan
text: Motivation. erplots originally binary-response-three layers (quantile summary, data strip, VPC), even though model curve/ribbon group panel layers always response-type-agnostic. erglm gained gaussian/Gamma/poisson family support matching example columns (biomarker_change, ae_count, ae_duration alongside ae1/ae2), stopped hypothetical: fitting gaussian model piping er_plot_show_quantiles() silently mis-plotted (error bars pinned y = 0/1) rather erroring. Decisions made: - response_type = c("auto", "binary", "continuous", "count") er_plot()/er_vpc_plot(). "auto" detects "binary" (logical values confined {0, 1}) vs. "continuous", never resolves "count" – ’s opt-, ’s purely additive existing auto-detected behaviour. - Continuous-response quantile bins use t-interval mean (bootstrap), matching inference convention erglm uses dispersion-estimated families. - Count responses default treated "continuous" (mean + t-interval) even "auto", documented known approximation; response_type = "count" explicit opt-fast-follow swaps exact Poisson/Garwood interval (poisson_interval()), (unlike t-interval) never produces negative lower bound low-count bin. - data strip (er_plot_show_datastrip(), named ) generalised pass – binary “responders line” two-panel design direct continuous analogue, left erroring loudly continuous/count responses rather guessing design. (Superseded later – see next section.) changed: er_plot() response-type detection/storage scale-aware corner-distance placement model layer’s p-value label; .part_quantile()/build_quantile_errorbar() dispatch response type (rate+Clopper-Pearson / mean+t-interval / mean+exact- Poisson); er_vpc_plot() gained dispatch observed-side summary; shared .abort_continuous_unsupported() guard added first (Stage 5) nothing silently mis-plot generalisation landed layer layer, removed call site layer generalised (now callers – see ). Status: done, devtools::check() clean, full test coverage including continuous/count fixtures (er_test_mod_gaussian, er_test_mod_poisson tests/testthat/helper-data.R), vignettes/articles/plot.Rmd’s “Continuous responses” section (including synthetic low-count example demonstrating t-interval’s negative-lower-bound failure mode vs. exact Poisson interval’s non-negative one).

title: erplots development plan
text: Motivation. pass’s “continuous variant” call data strip explicitly left open revisiting given concrete use case; section revisit. Naming decision (landed ahead redesign): “data strip” -> “data layer” throughout (er_plot_show_datastrip() -> er_plot_show_data(), .part_strip() -> .part_data(), build_datastrip_jitter() -> build_data_jitter(), object$part/plot$strip -> $data, etc.), matching existing model/quantile/group “named shows” convention. Design chosen continuous/count variant: single panel, points jittered along y = 0 , color mapped continuously response value rather partitioning upper/lower panels binary flag. Rejected alternatives: threshold-split two-panel design (rejected – threshold, e.g. median, arbitrary -motivated choice every continuous response) quantile-binned rug (rejected first pass using vertical space, noted reasonable fallback color encoding proves hard read practice). second, now-default design added alongside : rather separate panel, build_data_overlay() plots raw points directly base model panel true (exposure, response) coordinates – response-type-agnostic (amount jitter differs: small vertical nudge binary 0/1 values, none otherwise), color aesthetic (stratified) always strata, sharing base plot’s legend. er_plot_show_data()’s style argument defaults "overlay"; style = "jitter" selects older panel-based design. two styles mutually exclusive (object$part$data vs. object$part$overlay). Mechanics forced open: - strip composition machinery (.build_strip_plot(), .polish_margins()/.polish_arrangement()/.polish_labels()) hardcoded exactly two named panels (upper/lower). Generalised named list zero--panels (keyed stratum level continuous/count facet fallback), panel build order/vertical position (""/"") looked rather hardcoded. - Color longer reliably means “strata” every layer. config$color_role tag ("strata" vs. "response", set .part_data()) tells .polish_labels()/.polish_legends() whether part’s legend shared, deduplicated strata legend standalone response colorbar – consulted instead inferring meaning stratify alone. - generalises one-sentence rule, now documented ?er_plot’s “Stratification” section, ?er_plot_show_data, ?er_partial, vignettes/articles/design.Rmd: layer’s encoding takes precedence; stratification adapts whatever channel left, defaulting color/fill falling back per-stratum facets color already spoken . Today data layer’s style = "jitter" path (continuous/count) needs facet fallback. Status: done – composition refactor, build_data_color() + dispatch, build_data_overlay() + new default style, vignette updates (plot.Rmd’s “Data component” section covers styles side side) landed. devtools::check() clean throughout. Open questions, yet decided (flagging, blocking): - Color scale. Every builder package relies ggplot2’s default discrete hue scale explicit scale_color_*() call. build_data_color()’s continuous colorbar uses default scale_colour_gradient(), may read lower- contrast deliberately chosen scale (e.g. scale_colour_viridis_c()) – worth deliberate decision rather defaulting one. - Legibility scale. single continuous color gradient jittered 1-D strip fairly weak channel reading exact response values – inherent chosen design, bug, worth sanity check real dataset turns matter practice.

Status: done. vignettes/articles/design.Rmd carries closing note must updated change future grammar-altering decision (rename, new response-type dispatch, change singleton/additive status, etc.) – treat design change isn’t reflected incomplete.

what: Building a plot is composing layers onto an object
title: The plotting grammar
text: er_plot() creates empty object class er_plot, storing data plot’s exposure/response/stratification variables. pipe one layer functions, adds one visual component, finish plot()/print() ([er_plot_build()] directly):  currently four layers, documented help topic:
code: mod <- erglm_model(ae1 ~ aucss, erglm_data, family = binomial())  erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, ae1) |>   er_plot_show_model(mod) |>   er_plot_show_quantiles() |>   er_plot_show_groups(aucss) |>   plot() observed data       |       v   er_plot()                       -- creates the (empty) object       |       v   layer functions (piped, any order, any subset):     er_plot_show_model()     er_plot_show_quantiles()     er_plot_show_data()     er_plot_show_groups()       |       v   er_plot_build()                 -- called for you by plot()/print()       |       v   polish: margins, labels, legends, theme       |       v   compose: patchwork       |       v   rendered plot

what: Layers are either singleton or additive
title: The plotting grammar
text: Calling layer function twice object doesn’t always thing. model, quantile, data layers singleton: second call replaces first call’s result rather combining two. group layer one exception: ’s additive. call adds another panel alongside already added, rather replacing : deliberate design choice, oversight: one “model” one “quantile summary” show per plot, many legitimate ways slice exposure distribution different grouping variables. one flagged exception watch : model layer singleton, overlaying two model curves comparison (e.g. candidate model reference model) isn’t currently possible. ’s tracked plausible future addition, planned work – see PLAN.md.
code: plt <- erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, ae1) |>   er_plot_show_quantiles(bins = 4) |>   er_plot_show_quantiles(bins = 8) # overwrites the bins = 4 call  plt$part$quantile$config$n_quantiles # 8, not 4 #> [1] 8 plt <- erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, ae1) |>   er_plot_show_groups(aucss) |>   er_plot_show_groups(treatment) # adds a second panel, doesn't replace the first  names(plt$part$group$config) # both grouping variables are still there #> [1] "treatment"

what: Stratification composes with layers, usually via color
title: The plotting grammar
text: stratify_by, set er_plot(), declares single discrete variable used split layers color/fill, one shared, deduplicated legend across whole composed plot:  layer’s keep_strata argument controls whether layer uses stratification (default TRUE whenever stratify_by set). general rule, order layer actually applies : layer’s encoding takes precedence; stratification adapts whatever channel left, defaulting color/fill. layers, color/fill always free strata, rule invisible practice. data layer one exception, behaviour now depends style: style = "overlay" (default, build_data_overlay()): color, mapped , always means strata – response already shown via y-position, color/fill free stratification like every layer, overlay shares base plot’s strata legend model/quantile layers. style = "jitter" (older, panel-based design): binary response, build_data_jitter() behaves way – color means strata, shared legend. continuous/count response, build_data_color()’s color aesthetic already spoken response value , stratification falls back one panel per stratum level (colored response) instead shared legend. concrete instance “layer’s encoding takes precedence” motivated general rule – see PLAN.md’s “Continuous-response data strip” section design history, [er_plot_show_data()] full per-style breakdown. config$color_role tag ("strata" "response", set .part_data()) records meaning applies given data-layer build, composition machinery (.polish_labels()/ .polish_legends()) knows whether treat builder’s legend shared strata legend standalone response colorbar.
code: mod_strat <- erglm_model(ae1 ~ aucss + sex, erglm_data, family = binomial())  erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, ae1, stratify_by = sex) |>   er_plot_show_model(mod_strat) |>   er_plot_show_quantiles() |>   er_plot_show_data() |>   plot()

what: Response type changes what a layer summarises, not whether it appears
title: The plotting grammar
text: response_type, also set er_plot() ("auto", "binary", "continuous", "count"), governs response’s scale summary/CI method response-type-aware layer uses. model group layers don’t look response’s type – consume [er_predict()]/[er_simulate()] output exposure variable, respectively. quantile layer dispatches directly: "auto" classifies response "binary" ’s logical confined {0, 1}, "continuous" otherwise – genuine count response auto-detects "continuous" summarised approximately-continuous quantity unless response_type = "count" declared explicitly. See [er_plot_show_quantiles()] full rationale Plotting article’s “Continuous responses” section worked examples, including case choice t-interval exact Poisson interval visibly matters. data layer doesn’t compute summary statistic – just plots raw observations – response_type instead changes ’s drawn: style = "overlay" needs dispatch (plain scatter, small vertical jitter binary response’s exactly-0/1 y-values); style = "jitter" dispatches directly, choosing build_data_jitter()’s upper/lower panel split "binary" versus build_data_color()’s single response-colored panel (one per stratum) "continuous"/"count" – see [er_plot_show_data()].

what: Extending erplots: writing your own builder
title: The plotting grammar
text: Every layer function delegates actual drawing build_*() function sharing common signature – function(data, config, stratify, exposure, response, strata, style) – selected internally style (e.g. "errorbar" selects build_quantile_errorbar()). signature documented, public part API (see [er_partial()]), layer function’s builder argument (er_plot_show_model() additionally summary_builder) lets supply function place whatever style otherwise select – need fork package reach object$part internals:  custom builder receives pre-computed config built-builder (e.g. config$summary quantile layer, config$predictions model layer), needs turn config ggplot2 layers, recompute anything. data layer, style keeps structural meaning even builder supplied: "overlay" slots builder single call merged onto main panel, "jitter" slots one--panels stacked base plot (per object$response$type). three layers one structural call site, style simply ignored builder supplied. build_quantile_pointrange() (style = "pointrange", single geom_pointrange() place build_quantile_errorbar()’s separate point + error bar) started life exactly kind custom builder, promoted built-style option proved need new config fields – reasonable bar check custom builders , ’re deciding whether propose one upstream. See @examples [er_plot_show_model()], [er_plot_show_quantiles()], [er_plot_show_data()] worked custom builders (dashed model curve data-overlay scatter), [er_partial()]’s “Writing builder” section full contract.
code: build_quantile_crossbar <- function(data, config, stratify, exposure, response, strata, style) {   ggplot2::geom_crossbar(     data = config$summary,     mapping = ggplot2::aes(x = x_mid, y = y_mid, ymin = ci_lower, ymax = ci_upper),     inherit.aes = FALSE   ) }  erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, ae1) |>   er_plot_show_model(mod) |>   er_plot_show_quantiles(builder = build_quantile_crossbar) |>   plot()

title: Plotting

code: mod <- erglm_model(ae1 ~ aucss, erglm_data, family = binomial())

title: Plotting
text: Basic usage  Adding extra components
code: erglm_data |>    er_plot(exposure = aucss, response = ae1) |>    er_plot_show_model(mod) |>    er_plot_show_quantiles() |>    plot() erglm_data |>    er_plot(exposure = aucss, response = ae1) |>    er_plot_show_model(mod) |>    er_plot_show_quantiles() |>   er_plot_show_data() |>   er_plot_show_groups(group_by = aucss) |>    plot()

title: Plotting
text: Stratification adds colour across components. requires model includes stratification variable term:  can suppress stratification specific components
code: mod_strat <- erglm_model(ae1 ~ aucss + sex, erglm_data, family = binomial())  erglm_data |>    er_plot(     exposure = aucss,      response = ae1,      stratify_by = sex   ) |>    er_plot_show_model(mod_strat) |>    er_plot_show_quantiles() |>    er_plot_show_data() |>   plot() erglm_data |>    er_plot(     exposure = aucss,      response = ae1,      stratify_by = sex   ) |>    # keep_strata = FALSE needs a model that doesn't include the   # stratification variable, so we pass the un-stratified `mod` here   er_plot_show_model(mod, keep_strata = FALSE) |>    er_plot_show_quantiles() |>    er_plot_show_data() |>   plot()

text: Default style = "ribbonline" can also draw spaghetti plots represent parameter uncertainty. Spaghetti plots require model implement er_simulate() (erglm’s models ); models implement er_predict() fall back "ribbonline" message.

text: can modify number bins:  can also modify confidence level interval around bin’s summary. binary response Clopper-Pearson interval response rate:

title: Plotting
text: er_plot() auto-detects whether response binary (logical, values entirely {0, 1}) continuous, can override detection response_type. model component quantile component adapt: quantile bins report mean t-interval instead rate Clopper-Pearson interval. data layer component continuous-response variant (see ), ’s omitted .  er_vpc_plot() generalises way, comparing observed vs. simulated means rather rates:  Count (Poisson-style) responses, adverse-event count, auto-detected "continuous" summarised way (bin mean plus t-interval) unless declare response_type = "count" explicitly:  Declaring response_type = "count" swaps t-interval approximation exact Poisson interval (bin mean plus [poisson_interval()] instead [t_interval()]), never produces negative lower bound – useful low-count bins, t-interval approximation can. erglm_data’s ae_count, none bin means low enough actually happen, two plots look almost identical re-ran last one response_type = "count". make difference concrete, ’s synthetic dataset placebo arm 2 events among 20 subjects: default (t-interval) path’s placebo-arm error bar dips visibly zero – nonsensical negative event rate:   Declaring response_type = "count" keeps placebo-arm point estimate replaces t-interval exact Poisson interval, stays non-negative:   declaration works er_vpc_plot():
code: mod_gaussian <- erglm_model(biomarker_change ~ aucss, erglm_data, family = gaussian())  erglm_data |>    er_plot(aucss, biomarker_change) |>    er_plot_show_model(mod_gaussian) |>    er_plot_show_quantiles() |>    plot() sim_gaussian <- erglm_vpc_sim(mod_gaussian, seed = 3947) er_vpc_plot(erglm_data, sim_gaussian, aucss, biomarker_change, group_by = aucss) mod_poisson <- erglm_model(ae_count ~ aucss, erglm_data, family = poisson())  erglm_data |>    er_plot(aucss, ae_count) |>    er_plot_show_model(mod_poisson) |>    er_plot_show_quantiles() |>    plot() set.seed(84) placebo_counts <- rpois(20, 0.05) while (sum(placebo_counts) != 2) placebo_counts <- rpois(20, 0.05)  aucss_dosed <- sort(runif(80, 100, 3000)) count_dosed <- rpois(80, 0.1 + 0.003 * aucss_dosed)  low_count_data <- data.frame(   aucss = c(rep(0, 20), aucss_dosed),   ae_count = c(placebo_counts, count_dosed) )  mod_low_count <- erglm_model(ae_count ~ aucss, low_count_data, family = poisson()) low_count_data |>    er_plot(aucss, ae_count) |>    er_plot_show_model(mod_low_count) |>    er_plot_show_quantiles() |>    plot() +   ggplot2::coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-0.5, 1.5), xlim = c(-50, 800)) #> Coordinate system already present. #> ℹ Adding new coordinate system, which will replace the existing one. low_count_data |>    er_plot(aucss, ae_count, response_type = "count") |>    er_plot_show_model(mod_low_count) |>    er_plot_show_quantiles() |>    plot() +   ggplot2::coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-0.5, 1.5), xlim = c(-50, 800)) #> Coordinate system already present. #> ℹ Adding new coordinate system, which will replace the existing one. sim_poisson <- erglm_vpc_sim(mod_poisson, seed = 6142) er_vpc_plot(   erglm_data, sim_poisson, aucss, ae_count, group_by = aucss,   response_type = "count" )

title: Plotting
text: style = "jitter" older, panel-based design: binary response splits responders/non-responders separate jittered panels /main plot; continuous/count response draws single panel (one per stratum level) main plot, points colored continuously response value instead split panels. Unlike overlay, color aesthetic isn’t available stratification continuous/count response, since response value already claims . Building styles placing side side (via patchwork’s | operator) makes difference concrete binary response:  continuous response, style = "jitter" falls back single response-colored panel model curve rather overlay :  Stratification also looks different two styles: overlay’s color aesthetic always means strata shares model curve’s legend, regardless response type, whereas jitter’s color aesthetic means strata binary response (continuous/count falls back one titled panel per stratum level, response colorbar):
code: p_overlay <- erglm_data |>    er_plot(aucss, ae1) |>    er_plot_show_model(mod) |>    er_plot_show_quantiles() |>    er_plot_show_data(style = "overlay") |>    er_plot_build()  p_jitter <- erglm_data |>    er_plot(aucss, ae1) |>    er_plot_show_model(mod) |>    er_plot_show_quantiles() |>    er_plot_show_data(style = "jitter") |>    er_plot_build()  p_overlay$output | p_jitter$output p_overlay_cont <- erglm_data |>    er_plot(aucss, biomarker_change) |>    er_plot_show_model(mod_gaussian) |>    er_plot_show_data(style = "overlay") |>    er_plot_build()  p_jitter_cont <- erglm_data |>    er_plot(aucss, biomarker_change) |>    er_plot_show_model(mod_gaussian) |>    er_plot_show_data(style = "jitter") |>    er_plot_build()  p_overlay_cont$output | p_jitter_cont$output p_overlay_strat <- erglm_data |>    er_plot(aucss, ae1, stratify_by = sex) |>    er_plot_show_model(mod_strat) |>    er_plot_show_data(style = "overlay") |>    er_plot_build()  p_jitter_strat <- erglm_data |>    er_plot(aucss, ae1, stratify_by = sex) |>    er_plot_show_model(mod_strat) |>    er_plot_show_data(style = "jitter") |>    er_plot_build()  p_overlay_strat$output | p_jitter_strat$output

title: Model-Agnostic Exposure-Response Plots
text: erplots provides fluent mini-language building exposure-response plots: model curves/ribbons, quantile-binned response-rate summaries, data strips, grouped distribution panels. model-agnostic: erplots never fits model . Instead, fit model whatever package suits workflow (e.g. erglm logistic regression), pass fitted object er_plot_show_model(). model implements er_predict() can visualised; implementing er_simulate() er_summary() additionally enables uncertainty spaghetti plots/VPCs summary annotations (e.g. p-values). See ?er_model_interface.

what: Model interface for exposure-response plots — er_model_interface
title: Model interface for exposure-response plots — er_model_interface
text: erplots draws exposure-response plots fitted model implements small interface, rather assuming particular model class (e.g. logistic regression glm). make model usable er_plot_show_model() friends, implement least method er_predict(). Implementing er_simulate() additionally enables simulation-based visualisations (e.g. spaghetti plots, VPCs); implementing er_summary() enables annotations p-value labels.

title: Model interface for exposure-response plots — er_model_interface
code: er_predict(model, newdata, conf_level = 0.95, ...)  er_simulate(model, newdata, nsim = 100, seed = NULL, ...)  er_summary(model, ...)

title: Model interface for exposure-response plots — er_model_interface
text: model fitted exposure-response model object newdata data frame covariate values predict conf_level Confidence level prediction interval ... Passed methods nsim Number simulation replicates seed Optional RNG seed

title: Model interface for exposure-response plots — er_model_interface
text: er_predict() returns newdata three additional columns: fit_resp (point prediction), ci_lower, ci_upper. er_simulate() returns data frame containing nsim replicates newdata, sim_id column identifying replicate, fit_resp column giving simulated prediction replicate (reflecting parameter uncertainty). Models support simulation-based visualisation implement method; default method returns NULL; callers treat NULL result "available" rather error. er_summary() returns named list scalar summary statistics (example list(p_value = 0.013)), NULL nothing available. default method returns NULL.

See er_plot()'s "Layers either singleton additive" section full discussion, including one flagged future exception (additive model layer, overlaying two fitted curves). data slot two mutually exclusive builders, selected response type rather name: build_data_jitter() binary response (color, mapped, always means strata) build_data_color() continuous/count response (color always means response value ). build_data_color()'s color aesthetic already spoken , config$color_role (set .part_data(), consulted .polish_labels()/.polish_legends() R/er-plot-compose.R) tags meaning applies – "strata" build_data_jitter(), "response" build_data_color() – composition machinery knows whether builder's legend shared strata legend standalone response colorbar. build_data_overlay() needs tag: color aesthetic (stratified) always strata, since response already shown via y-position, shares base plot's strata legend directly. title: The exposure-response plotting mini-language — er_plot
text: data Observed data exposure Exposure variable (one variable, unquoted) response Response variable (one variable, unquoted) stratify_by Stratification variable used color fill (one variable, unquoted); see "Stratification" response_type One "auto" (default), "binary", "continuous", "count". Governs response-scale defaults (e.g. axis limits) summary/CI method quantile VPC layers use. "auto", response column classified "binary" logical takes values {0, 1}, "continuous" otherwise – means count (Poisson-style) response, e.g. adverse-event count, auto-detects "continuous" (counts confined {0, 1}) summarised approximately-continuous quantity (bin mean plus t-interval). "auto" never resolves "count": pass response_type = "count" explicitly genuine count response instead get bin mean plus exact Poisson interval (see poisson_interval()), – unlike t-interval approximation – never produces negative lower bound. See PLAN.md's design decision (4) rationale.

title: The exposure-response plotting mini-language — er_plot
text: er_plot_show_model(), er_plot_show_quantiles(), er_plot_show_data() singleton: calling one twice object overwrites first call's result rather combining two. er_plot_show_groups() additive: call adds another grouped-distribution panel alongside already added, rather replacing . asymmetry deliberate, accidental – one "model" one "quantile summary" show per plot, many legitimate ways slice exposure distribution different grouping variables. See PLAN.md's "Mini-language architecture review" design discussion, including one flagged future exception: overlaying two model curves comparison currently supported, one singleton layer additive variant might eventually make sense.

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(erglm) mod2 <- erglm_model(ae2 ~ aucss + sex, erglm_data, family = binomial()) erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, ae2, stratify_by = sex) |>   er_plot_show_model(mod2, keep_strata = FALSE) |>   er_plot_show_quantiles() |>   er_plot_show_data() |>   plot()  # continuous response: overlay works the same way, with no # response-type-specific styling needed mod3 <- erglm_model(biomarker_change ~ aucss, erglm_data, family = gaussian()) erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, biomarker_change) |>   er_plot_show_model(mod3) |>   er_plot_show_data() |>   plot()  # older panel-based design: a single color-encoded panel below the # base plot, instead of an overlay in the main panel erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, biomarker_change) |>   er_plot_show_model(mod3) |>   er_plot_show_data(style = "jitter") |>   plot()  # plug in a 2D density in the main panel instead of a scatter; `style` # still picks the "overlay" (single main-panel) layout -- see `?er_partial` build_data_density <- function(data, config, stratify, exposure, response, strata, style) {   ggplot2::geom_density_2d(     data = data,     mapping = ggplot2::aes(x = .data[[exposure$name]], y = .data[[response$name]])   ) } erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, biomarker_change) |>   er_plot_show_model(mod3) |>   er_plot_show_data(builder = build_data_density) |>   plot() } # }

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(erglm) mod <- erglm_model(ae1 ~ aucss, erglm_data, family = binomial()) erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, ae1) |>   er_plot_show_model(mod) |>   plot()  # plug in a custom model-curve builder instead of choosing a built-in # `style`; see `?er_partial` for the full contract build_model_dashed <- function(data, config, stratify, exposure, response, strata, style) {   ggplot2::geom_line(     data = config$predictions,     mapping = ggplot2::aes(x = .data[[exposure$name]], y = fit_resp),     linetype = "dashed"   ) } erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, ae1) |>   er_plot_show_model(mod, builder = build_model_dashed) |>   plot() } # }

title: Add a quantile-binned response summary layer — er_plot_show_quantiles
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(erglm) mod <- erglm_model(ae1 ~ aucss, erglm_data, family = binomial()) erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, ae1) |>   er_plot_show_model(mod) |>   er_plot_show_quantiles() |>   plot()  # continuous response: bin means/t-intervals instead of rates/ # Clopper-Pearson intervals, auto-detected from the response column mod3 <- erglm_model(biomarker_change ~ aucss, erglm_data, family = gaussian()) erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, biomarker_change) |>   er_plot_show_model(mod3) |>   er_plot_show_quantiles() |>   plot()  # count response: declare response_type = "count" explicitly for an # exact Poisson interval instead of the t-interval approximation used # by the auto-detected ("continuous") default mod4 <- erglm_model(ae_count ~ aucss, erglm_data, family = poisson()) erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, ae_count, response_type = "count") |>   er_plot_show_model(mod4) |>   er_plot_show_quantiles() |>   plot()  # a pointrange instead of an errorbar erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, ae1) |>   er_plot_show_model(mod) |>   er_plot_show_quantiles(style = "pointrange") |>   plot()  # plug in a fully custom builder instead of choosing a built-in # `style`; see `?er_partial` for the full contract build_quantile_crossbar <- function(data, config, stratify, exposure, response, strata, style) {   ggplot2::geom_crossbar(     data = config$summary,     mapping = ggplot2::aes(x = x_mid, y = y_mid, ymin = ci_lower, ymax = ci_upper),     inherit.aes = FALSE   ) } erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, ae1) |>   er_plot_show_model(mod) |>   er_plot_show_quantiles(builder = build_quantile_crossbar) |>   plot() } # }

what: Visual predictive check plot for an exposure-response model — er_vpc_plot
title: Visual predictive check plot for an exposure-response model — er_vpc_plot
text: Compares observed response rates simulated response rates model, stratified grouping variable. function model-agnostic: operates purely data frames. sim data frame expected contain one row per simulated observation per replicate, sim_id column identifying replicate (see e.g. erglm::erglm_vpc_sim() one way generate simulations fitted model).

title: Visual predictive check plot for an exposure-response model — er_vpc_plot
code: er_vpc_plot(   data,   sim,   exposure,   response,   group_by,   conf_level = 0.95,   response_type = c("auto", "binary", "continuous", "count") )

title: Visual predictive check plot for an exposure-response model — er_vpc_plot
text: data Observed data sim Simulated data, exposure/response/group_by columns data, plus sim_id column identifying replicate exposure Exposure variable (one variable, unquoted) response Response variable (one variable, unquoted). May binary (0/1, logical) continuous; see response_type group_by Variable (unquoted) stratify predictions conf_level Confidence level response_type One "auto" (default), "binary", "continuous", "count". Governs observed-side summary computed: response rate Clopper-Pearson CI "binary", bin mean t-interval "continuous" (see t_interval()), bin mean exact Poisson interval "count" (see poisson_interval()). "auto" detects observed response column (entirely {0, 1}, logical, treated binary; see er_plot()'s response_type heuristic) never resolves "count": count (Poisson-style) response auto-detects "continuous" (counts confined {0, 1}) summarised bin-mean-plus-t-interval approximation unless response_type = "count" declared explicitly, case exact Poisson interval used instead – see PLAN.md's design decision (4) rationale.

title: Visual predictive check plot for an exposure-response model — er_vpc_plot
text: ggplot2 object

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(erglm) mod <- erglm_model(ae2 ~ aucss + sex, erglm_data, family = binomial()) sim <- erglm_vpc_sim(mod) er_vpc_plot(erglm_data, sim, aucss, ae2, group_by = aucss) er_vpc_plot(erglm_data, sim, aucss, ae2, group_by = sex)  mod_gaussian <- erglm_model(biomarker_change ~ aucss, erglm_data, family = gaussian()) sim_gaussian <- erglm_vpc_sim(mod_gaussian) er_vpc_plot(erglm_data, sim_gaussian, aucss, biomarker_change, group_by = aucss)  mod_poisson <- erglm_model(ae_count ~ aucss, erglm_data, family = poisson()) sim_poisson <- erglm_vpc_sim(mod_poisson) er_vpc_plot(   erglm_data, sim_poisson, aucss, ae_count, group_by = aucss,   response_type = "count" ) } # }

title: Exact Poisson confidence interval for a count rate — poisson_interval
text: x Vector (sum) observed counts, e.g. counts falling one exposure bin n Number units counts accumulated (e.g. number observations bin); rate estimated sum(x) / n conf_level Confidence level

title: Exact Poisson confidence interval for a count rate — poisson_interval
text: Named numeric vector (lower, upper) rate sum(x) / n, confidence level stored attribute. Uses standard exact ("Garwood") Poisson interval, derived chi-squared/gamma relationship, rather normal approximation. total count 0, lower bound 0 (gamma quantile shape = 0).

title: Exact Poisson confidence interval for a count rate — poisson_interval
text: count-response analogue clopper_pearson(), used quantile-binned summary layer (see er_plot_show_quantiles()) er_vpc_plot() response_type = "count" explicitly declared. Unlike t_interval() (default, opt--required approximation used count response auto-detects declared "continuous"), interval exact never produces negative lower bound – see PLAN.md design decision (4) rationale history.

title: Exact Poisson confidence interval for a count rate — poisson_interval
code: poisson_interval(3, 10) #>      lower      upper  #> 0.06186721 0.87672731  #> attr(,"conf_level") #> [1] 0.95
